topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, featuring a curated collection of prompt examples that work with various AI chat models, including ChatGPT, Claude, and more.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide that covers over 25 chapters on prompt engineering, ranging from basic techniques to advanced methods like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the 'Prompting for Kids' section?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' section is designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through interactive, game-based adventures that include fun puzzles and stories.

entries.q: What are the requirements for self-hosting prompts.chat?
entries.a: To self-host prompts.chat, you need to run a setup command using npx or clone the GitHub repository, install dependencies, and configure branding, themes, and authentication, with PostgreSQL recommended as the database.

entries.q: How can I use prompts.chat as an MCP server?
entries.a: You can use prompts.chat as an MCP server by configuring it in your AI tools with either a remote URL or a local command setup, as detailed in the MCP documentation.

entries.q: What notable recognition has prompts.chat received?
entries.a: Prompts.chat has been featured in Forbes, referenced by Harvard and Columbia, and has over 40 academic citations, making it a well-recognized resource in the AI community.
